assertions.reason: The central audit log route crashes with `column audit_logs.ip_address does not exist` before any audit entries render, so the UI never lets a user verify create/update/delete audit entries or their immutability.
The same audit log schema error prevents the central audit log from rendering its search/filter UI, and I did not find any entity-specific audit surface on the implemented foundation routes.

The notification centre showed newest-first notifications with an unread badge, individual mark-as-read reduced the unread count, bulk mark-as-read cleared the badge, and a notification source link navigated to the team page.

Opening the broken audit log route showed the route error boundary while the dashboard shell stayed usable; Retry kept the user in the contained fallback state and Go to dashboard recovered to the main dashboard.
Navigating from the dashboard to Team displayed the org-scoped skeleton loader with the sidebar and header still visible before the page resolved, and the flow produced no console/runtime errors.

frictions.description: After onboarding completed, the browser remained on `/onboarding` until I refreshed the session with active-org context. I stayed within scope by continuing with only the assigned account/org and seeding the minimal notification/audit data allowed by the task.
frictions.resolution: Generated a session for the assigned owner account with its active org and reopened the dashboard before continuing UI validation.

frictions.description: agent-browser's request log did not expose the Next.js server-action calls behind notification read-state changes.
frictions.resolution: Validated notification behaviour via visible unread badge/count changes, list state updates, and successful source-link navigation instead.

blockers.description: The live `audit_logs` table is missing the `ip_address` column expected by the audit log queries, so the central audit UI trips its error boundary on every visit.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Retried from the route error boundary and reopened `/dashboardaudit-org-20260401-foundation/audit-log` directly; both reproduced the same error.

notes: Validated using the assigned owner account on org `dashboardaudit-org-20260401-foundation` only.
No entity-specific audit history surface was discoverable on the foundation routes exposed by the dashboard shell (dashboard, settings, team, audit log).
Console/runtime errors were clean for notification and loading-state flows; the audit route consistently logged the `audit_logs.ip_address` schema error.
